result: **Goal: Add a Magnetic Ising Model spin simulation mode** — a statistical mechanics system where cells hold +1/−1 spins that flip based on neighbor alignment and a user-controllable temperature parameter. At low temperature, large ordered domains form; at high temperature, random noise dominates. Near the critical temperature, fractal-like domain boundaries emerge. The user can raise/lower temperature in real-time with keys and watch the phase transition unfold — one of the most visually dramatic phenomena in physics, and a fundamentally different class of simulation (energy minimization with thermal noise) compared to everything currently in the project.
